$comment: Canonical MCP tools/list manifest for the Cipher & Row freight verification MCP server. This is the exact public Phase 1 tool surface an MCP client receives from https://mcp.cipherandrow.com/mcp. Generated for documentation; the live server is the source of truth. Server: cipher-and-row v2.1.0, MCP protocol 2025-06-18. Descriptions/inputSchemas last synced against a live tools/list call on 2026-07-12.

tools.name: cr_verify_carrier
tools.description: Fetch a US carrier's live FMCSA profile by DOT or MC number, at full dashboard parity. Returns the complete carrier profile (operating status, authority history, fleet, insurance detail incl. BIPD/cargo/bond flags + expiry, safety rating, 24-month inspections and crashes, complaints, MCS-150, cargo/operation classifications, contact + addresses), fraud/identity signals - fraud_signals: the same 7-signal set the dashboard's fraud card shows (sanctions, industry fraud reports, operating authority, out-of-service, BIPD insurance, safety record, chameleon/reincarnation check), plus raw double_broker_signals hits and advisory_flags - a legacy risk_assessment (PROCEED/CAUTION/BLOCK), AND a canonical 0-100 trust_score with per-factor breakdown + breakdown_max + data_confidence (the same score the dashboard surfaces). trust_score is null when this carrier has never been looked up across any CR surface. EIN is deliberately excluded from the agent door. FMCSA result cached 24h; trust_score and the fraud/identity signals are read fresh on every call. Requires a DOT or MC number - if you only have a legal name, call cr_search_registry first to resolve the identifier. Not available to carrier-role accounts.

dot_number.description: The carrier's USDOT number (provide this or mc_number).

mc_number.description: The carrier's MC number (provide this or dot_number).

tools.name: cr_verify_broker
tools.description: Fetch a freight broker's verified profile and canonical 0-100 trust_score (with per-factor breakdown + breakdown_max + data_confidence), at full dashboard parity. US brokers (mc_number or dot_number): live FMCSA profile, authority status, full surety bond detail (broker.bond: BMC-84/85 type, surety company, amount, effective/cancellation dates, classification flags, freshness), fraud/identity signals - fraud_signals: the same 7-signal set the dashboard's fraud card shows (sanctions, industry fraud reports, broker authority, bond, bond classification, payout/remit-to changes, tender blocks), plus raw double_broker_signals hits and advisory_flags - and risk_assessment. Canadian brokers: provide cab_id (e.g. CAB-ON-0000157) for the Cipher & Row Canadian registry profile (province, verification band, CTQ status) with the same canonical trust_score. If you only have a legal name, call cr_search_registry first to resolve the identifier.

mc_number.description: US broker MC number (provide one of mc_number / dot_number / cab_id).

dot_number.description: US broker DOT number (provide one of mc_number / dot_number / cab_id).

cab_id.description: Canadian broker CAB number, format CAB-XX-NNNNNNN.

annotations.title: Verify broker (US FMCSA or Canadian registry)

tools.name: cr_search_registry
tools.description: Find carriers and brokers in the Cipher & Row registry by legal name or identifier (MC, USDOT, CAB, CVOR, CTQ, NSC, CR ID). Returns a list of matches with identity only - legal name, identifiers, country, and claim status - so you can pick the right entity. Trust scores are NOT returned here; call cr_verify_carrier or cr_verify_broker on a match to get its trust score (that is the verified, metered lookup).

query.description: Legal name or identifier (MC, USDOT, CR ID, etc.) - required.

entity_type.description: Filter by entity type. 'any' returns both.

tools.name: cr_session_status
tools.description: Introspect the MCP server: build version, calling agent identity, company logistics_role, subscription tier, per-category rate-limit headroom, and the list of tools available to this account type. Use this to detect tier ceilings and confirm which tools your role can call. Returns available_tools - the definitive role- and scope-filtered list your connection can call (computed with the same filter tools/list uses, so the two always agree) - plus granted_scopes so you can self-diagnose a missing-scope mismatch.

tools.name: cr_add_partner
tools.description: Add a carrier or broker to your partners watchlist. Cipher & Row monitors authority, insurance, bond, safety rating, address, and phone for material changes and notifies you via your chosen channels. Idempotent: re-adding the same partner updates the existing watch. Provide dot_number when partner_kind=carrier, mc_number when partner_kind=broker.

partner_kind.description: Carrier accounts may only add partner_kind=broker.

dot_number.description: The partner's USDOT number. Required when partner_kind=carrier.

mc_number.description: The partner's MC number. Required when partner_kind=broker.

legal_name.description: Partner legal name (optional; backfilled from FMCSA if omitted).

change_types.description: Which changes to be notified about. Default: the six standard types; chameleon_flagged (reincarnated-carrier alerts) is opt-in.

tools.name: cr_remove_partner
tools.description: Stop watching a partner. Soft-deletes the watch (active=false); past alert history is preserved. Reversible - re-add with cr_add_partner to resume watching.

partner_id.description: The partner id returned by cr_add_partner / cr_list_partners. Provide this or (partner_kind + dot_number/mc_number).

tools.name: cr_list_partners
tools.description: List your active partners watchlist with current status, last_event_at, and (if `since` is provided) the events that have fired since that timestamp. The hourly partner-watch-diff cron drives change detection.

since.description: ISO timestamp. If provided, response includes events fired after this time for each partner.

tools.name: cr_set_notifications
tools.description: Update notification preferences. With `partner_id`: tune one watch. Without: set the company default applied to future cr_add_partner calls. Returns the effective settings after the update.

partner_id.description: Optional. If omitted, updates the company-default policy on future watches.
